mentions.note: Don't take selling personally; Cramer loves Apple and says own it, don't trade it. Selling driven by speculators liquidating to raise SpaceX IPO cash. Apple spending next to nothing on AI via Alphabet deal.
Selling is driven by speculators raising cash for SpaceX/Anthropic/OpenAI IPOs, not company fundamentals. Own it, don't trade it. Minimal AI spending due to Alphabet deal.
Selling is from speculators raising cash for SpaceX; don't take it personally. Cramer loves Apple, owns it. It's a donor to SpaceX cause, not a weak fundamental story. Own it, don't trade it.

Selling is forced liquidation to fund SpaceX IPO, not fundamental weakness. Cramer loves Apple; own it, don't trade it. Used as liquidity source by speculators. Long-term holder play; ignore day-to-day noise.
Heavy selling driven by portfolio liquidation for SpaceX allocations, not fundamentals. AI spending minimal due to Alphabet/Gemini deal. Own long-term, don't trade.
Fantastic balance sheet, robust buyback, beloved products; still has scarcity characteristics despite broader tech oversupply fears.

Clean story but WWDC announcement disappointed traders. Siri improvements and AI integration real, but Apple's leadership position matters too much to lose. Wanted it to open down and rally instead; traders are the losers, not Apple.
Avoids massive AI capex by piggybacking off Alphabet's spending; with 2.5B active devices, competitive chatbot makers need access to Apple's audience. WWDC Monday should reveal Siri improvements.

Best hardware; snared Gemini AI (logical extension of Google partnership, which pays Apple fortune for search placement); thought behind in AI but users not switching handsets; smart Siri getting smarter; near-perfect execution drives ever-higher stock price and acclaim.
2016 plunge to $23 offered major discount for best-in-class products and customer loyalty; skeptics proved wrong repeatedly; example of why patience with quality pays off.
Still matters due to iPhone and App Store (software), but era of software dominance that made Apple valuable is ending as hardware/AI chips now drive value.
Added to FANG as FAANGu; Magnificent Seven member; obvious consumer tech; $1,000 in 2013 would have grown to $17,994 by 2024.
One of two stocks earning 'own it, don't trade it' highest blessing; revolutionary company with outstanding management; still monitor thesis changes.
Revenue up 17%, guidance good; Cramer says own it don't trade it; camera quality exceptional; previously misunderstood.
Reporting Q1 earnings Thursday night (day after mega-cap cluster); historically part of turning point in earnings season; watch guidance and iPhone demand trends.
Reports Thursday; doesn't need to spend heavily on data centers due to large user base and ability to lean on Google/Gemini. AI story less capital-intensive.
Tim Cook transitioning to John Turnus; doing exceptionally well; strong iPhone 17 line and foldables support continued strength; own it don't trade it.
Tim Cook stepping down; his consumer-centric philosophy created 1,900% stock gains and 43% buyback over tenure. Device has unique friendship quality; successor faces high bar.
Huge beneficiary of AI revolution for users; Tim Cook stepping down (John Turnus taking over in September) but leadership transition does not diminish AI opportunity.
Never really went anywhere; ready to roll as consumer demand stabilizes and services growth accelerates.
Eric's position; own it, don't trade it; premium valuation justified by ecosystem and services growth.

Nikkei Asia spread false foldable phone delay rumor; stock down 13 points; Cramer bought when down 10; Bloomberg confirmed on-time at 1pm; stock rallied 5 points. Recommend own, don't trade; no war tomorrow = fly higher.
Core holding for long-time investor (Chris) from pandemic low; strong performer; maintain position.

Blowout iPhone sales, China strength, strong gross margin guidance; avoided capex by partnering with Google on Gemini AI; up 8% since quarter; own don't trade; Charitable Trust and Travel Trust positions.
Making move on terrific guidance; pessimists losing narrative control; still has upside despite bear pressure.

Tremendous quarter with strong U.S. and shocking China recovery; iPhone revenue +22%; finished up $1.20 despite bearish sell-off on parts shortage fears; Cramer confident supply chain mastery and pricing power will overcome.
iPhone 17 sales crushed (85B vs. 79B forecast). Magnificent quarter top-and-bottom line. Own it, don't trade it.

Reports Thursday after 8-week losing streak. Memory chip cost pressure on margins key watch. iPhone 17 strong launch should show in quarter. Gemini AI partnership may net $20B+ annually. Only Mag 7 company avoiding massive CapEx — own it don't trade it.
Trading at one of highest P/E multiples in entire market. Great momentum but too much valuation danger — too much risk even for quality company at current multiples.
Down eight straight weeks due to storage cost pressures. Don't change view; own it, don't trade it, but respect margin pressure risk.
Household name with 20-year history of outperformance via disciplined stock picking; core Magnificent 7 holding despite P/E compression; money will return.
Weak performance partly due to memory chip costs eating into profit margins; component inflation pressure.
Mega-cap tech off 9% YTD despite strong 2024; could be another 8th week of downside; part of concerning rotation out of quality into froth.
Consumer electronics and tech exposure; large cap technology holding; growth with stability.
Stock flat despite expensive phones showing strength in TSMC data; buyers haven't figured out to buy yet; Cramer worried about lack of buyer interest.
Partnership with Alphabet to integrate AI into Siri; benefits from advanced AI capabilities.
Fundamentals unchanged; stock weakness reflects money managers using as source of funds to buy neglected names at year-start. Own, don't trade.

Up 9%, beaten down early 2025 on growth/China/tariff fears; tariff reversed via domestic investment push; iPhone 17 launch strong; avoided costly AI data-center buildout—suddenly look smart; 32x 2026 earnings (not cheap but deserves premium); possible big Gemini AI deal; Cramer 'own it, don't trade it.'
Down 4 bucks today; Cramer recommends buying on pullback, add if it drops further; competitive pressure but long-term strength remains.
